history_text: 53-year-old male with a 15-year history of Type 2 diabetes mellitus, currently moderately controlled (HbA1c 8.2%). Comorbidities include hyperlipidaemia. Renal function is mildly to moderately reduced (eGFR 65.4 mL/min/1.73m²). Current medications include Insulin NPH 22 units nightly, Amlodipine 5mg once daily, Atorvastatin 20mg nightly. No known drug allergies. He denies recent hospitalisations or surgical procedures.
